name: Pakistan
summary: Day 90 of Hormuz blockade (Thursday May 28). **NO NEW DEVELOPMENT MAY 28 — STATE OF PLAY UNCHANGED. Pakistan's May 26 on-the-record rejection of the Abraham Accords (DefMin Asif: 'clashes with our fundamental ideologies') stands; Islamabad has issued no further public response since. The Munir COAS mediation track + the Qatar dual-channel continue underneath; the Pakistan-framework-draft trigger holds at partially_active_leaked_text (by 2026-05-30). Carry-forward only.** PRIOR (May 27): **PAKISTAN'S DEFENCE MINISTER KHAWAJA MUHAMMAD ASIF PUBLICLY REJECTED JOINING THE ABRAHAM ACCORDS — 'CLASHES WITH OUR FUNDAMENTAL IDEOLOGIES' (CONFIRMED diplomatic action, via Samaa TV). This is the first CONFIRMED accept/reject of Trump's mandatory-Accords condition and it FIRES the trigger added May 25 on the rejection limb.** Trump (May 26 Truth Social) responded that the signatories 'should not be part of this Deal' and that refusal 'shows bad intention'; per a US official cited by Axios the leaders on the weekend call — 'especially those of Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Pakistan' — 'appeared stunned' (REPORTED, single Axios channel). The list of named nations also WIDENED May 26 from six to EIGHT (UAE + Bahrain added). The rejection raises the activation energy for any signature and hands every reluctant capital a fresh reason to slow-walk the Iran deal — while Pakistan's own substantive deliverable (the Munir COAS mediation track + the Qatar dual-channel) continues underneath. **Encode the rejection as a CONFIRMED diplomatic action, NOT mere slow-walk: Islamabad has now said no on the record.** PRIOR (May 25): **TRUMP NAMED PAKISTAN AMONG SIX MUSLIM NATIONS 'MANDATORILY' REQUIRED TO SIGN THE ABRAHAM ACCORDS AS PART OF THE IRAN DEAL — PLACING ISLAMABAD IN A 'DIFFICULT POSITION.' RESPONSE: SILENCE / SLOW-WALK.** Trump demanded six Muslim nations 'simultaneously' normalise with Israel; per Axios the leaders, 'especially those of Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Pakistan, who don't have formal diplomatic relations with Israel,' were surprised — 'silence on the line' [RHETORIC — no acceptance]. Pakistan historically backs a two-state solution and declined the Accords under Imran Khan, so the demand puts it in a structurally difficult position relative to its mediator role; encode as a watching/slow-walk signal, NOT acceptance. **The mandatory-Accords condition coexists with Pakistan's substantive deliverable**: the Munir COAS Tehran mediation track (concluded May 23, 'encouraging progress toward a final understanding') and the dual-channel architecture with Qatar (Qatari delegation now in Doha hosting Iran's Ghalibaf/Araghchi/Hemmati). The Pakistan-framework-draft trigger holds at partially_active_leaked_text (Day 9 of 14, by May 30); Trump's 'no rush' pivot extends the timeline. PRIOR (May 24): **MUNIR-TEHRAN VISIT OPERATIONALLY CONCLUDED SATURDAY MAY 23 — ISPR DECLARED 'ENCOURAGING PROGRESS TOWARD A FINAL UNDERSTANDING' BUT NO PUBLISHED FRAMEWORK TEXT YET. ABDOLLAHI MEETING (IRAN'S KHATAM AL-ANBIYA CENTRAL HQ COMMANDER) ADDED TO ITINERARY ELEVATES VISIT BEYOND CIVILIAN-MEDIATION FRAMING TO MILITARY-TO-MILITARY AXIS. PARALLEL QATARI NEGOTIATING TEAM IN TEHRAN SINCE FRIDAY MAY 22 — DUAL MEDIATION ARCHITECTURE (PAKISTAN FORMAL/MILITARY + QATAR SUBSTANTIVE/IRGC) NOW OPERATIONALLY EXPLICIT WITH PARALLEL DELEGATIONS. ISPR FRAMING NOT YET VALIDATED BY MATCHING IRANIAN-SIDE STATEMENT; BAGHAEI'S MORE GUARDED 'REDUCTION IN DISPUTES, BUT THERE ARE STILL ISSUES THAT NEED TO BE DISCUSSED THROUGH MEDIATORS' IS THE CLOSEST IRANIAN MATCH. NEXT OPERATIONAL READ: WHETHER VISIT CONVERTS TO A PUBLISHED FRAMEWORK TEXT WITHIN THE 14-DAY WINDOW (BY MAY 30, DAY 8 OF 14).** (1) **Munir COAS Tehran visit operationally concluded Saturday May 23** (CONFIRMED — Tribune India primary citing ISPR + ANI, Al Jazeera May 23, Tasnim, Press TV, Arab News). Met Pezeshkian + Ghalibaf + Araghchi + Khatam al-Anbiya Central HQ Commander Maj Gen Ali Abdollahi. Abdollahi meeting NOT on the May 22 pre-released ISPR itinerary — late addition that elevates the visit beyond civilian-mediation framing to military-to-military axis. (2) **ISPR readout (Pakistan-side framing, REPORTED)**: 'Negotiations over the past 24 hours have led to encouraging progress toward a final understanding.' Iranian-side matching framing notably absent — Baghaei's 'trend this week has been towards a reduction in disputes, but there are still issues that need to be discussed through mediators' is closest match but materially more guarded. **Pakistan FM Khawaja Asif separately described mediation as 'moving toward a positive outcome'** (Pakistan Today). (3) **Qatari negotiating team arrived in Tehran on Friday May 22** (REPORTED — Reuters via Investing.com, JPost) 'in coordination with the United States to try to help secure a deal' — parallel to Munir's Pakistan-COAS visit and pre-existing the Saturday Trump 'largely negotiated' announcement. Qatar's Emir Sheikh Tamim held calls with MbS and MbZ on regional developments (CONFIRMED — Türkiye Today, Yeni Safak). **Dual mediation architecture now operationally explicit with parallel delegations**: Pakistan = formal/military track (Munir COAS); Qatar = substantive/IRGC backchannel (Qatari team). Both delegations in Tehran simultaneously, suggesting the deal architecture deliberately bifurcates the political-public track from the substantive-text track. (4) **Pakistan-framework-draft trigger (Day 8 of 14, by 2026-05-30)** remains at partially_active_leaked_text. The Munir visit was the procedural action; the published framework text is the next deliverable. Trump's Sunday 'no rush' pivot operationally extends the timeline past the 14-day window. (5) **Pakistan's structural positioning intact**: COAS-level mediation is the cleanest Pakistan-side deliverable of the cycle and consistent with Pakistan's incentive structure (avoid regional war that would devastate Pakistan's energy economy and Gulf-remittance corridor). The dual-channel architecture with Qatar means Pakistan retains the OFFICIAL title and military-mediation visibility, while Qatar runs the SUBSTANTIVE backchannel via IRGC senior generals — Vali Nasr's framing 'Pakistan's role is unprecedented' operates on the OFFICIAL axis. PRIOR (May 23): PAKISTAN'S COAS FIELD MARSHAL ASIM MUNIR CONDUCTED A ~24-HOUR MEDIATION VISIT TO TEHRAN MAY 22-23 — MEDIATION TRACK STRUCTURALLY ESCALATED FROM INTERIOR MINISTER NAQVI TO MILITARY-CHIEF LEVEL, THE MOST POWERFUL PAKISTANI SINGLE ACTOR.** Munir met Iranian President Pezeshkian + Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf + FM Araghchi + Interior Minister Momeni (CONFIRMED — Al Jazeera May 22-23 primary, ARY News, Pakistan Observer/ISPR primary, Geo TV, Daily Pakistan, Times of Israel). ISPR described talks as 'short but highly productive' with 'encouraging progress towards a final understanding' on ending the war (REPORTED framing, ISPR primary). The visit is a **structural escalation** of the mediation track: through May 22 Pakistan's interface was Interior Minister Naqvi; Munir's arrival promotes the channel to the military-to-military axis Tehran trusts more than the civilian government. Al Jazeera May 23 quoted an Iranian official describing Qatar's role as 'vital, important, and fundamental to supporting Pakistan's mediation' — the dual-channel architecture (Pakistan formal/military + Qatar substantive/IRGC backchannel) is now operationally explicit. Saudi FM Faisal bin Farhan publicly praised Trump diplomacy + Pakistan mediation + urged Iran to 'seize the opportunity to avoid escalation' (Al Arabiya May 22-23) — Gulf editorial coordination intact with Saudi positioning for primary deal credit. **The Iranian-side hardening counter-signal: Ghalibaf publicly stated US is 'not an honest negotiating partner'; Iran UN mission accused Washington of 'excessive demands'; Tasnim reported (military source primary) 'Iran is preparing for resuming fighting with the United States and Israel.'** Iran's institutional fanout while Munir is in Tehran = coordinated multi-track posture (not factional split): civilian-government procedural + legislative-conservative rhetorical + military-IRGC kinetic-ready + supreme-leader nuclear-foreclosure. **The Pakistan-framework-draft trigger (Day 4 of 14, by 2026-05-30) remains at partially_active_leaked_text** — no formal US/Iran acceptance through May 23; Trump's 'every day better' + 'will likely decide tomorrow' framing keeps the May 24 strike-window live in parallel. PRIOR (May 22): **PAKISTAN-MEDIATED DRAFT DEAL TEXT LEAKED VIA AL ARABIYA LATE MAY 21 — CEASEFIRE / HORMUZ FREEDOM-OF-NAVIGATION / SANCTIONS-PHASING ARCHITECTURE, NO URANIUM DISMANTLEMENT. THE PAKISTAN MEDIATION CHANNEL IS NOW THE CLEANEST OPERATIONAL DELIVERABLE OF THE CYCLE.** Across May 20-22 Pakistan Interior Minister Naqvi met Iran FM Araghchi in Tehran multiple times (CONFIRMED — Washington Times May 22, IRNA primary). Saudi network Al Arabiya late May 21 leaked what it called the 'final draft' mediated by Pakistan, 'could be announced within hours': (i) immediate, comprehensive, unconditional ceasefire on all fronts (land, sea, air); (ii) mutual commitment not to target military, civilian or economic infrastructure; (iii) end to military operations and halt to 'media war'; (iv) commitments to respect sovereignty, territorial integrity, non-interference; (v) joint monitoring mechanism for ceasefire implementation; (vi) freedom-of-navigation guarantees through the Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz; (vii) negotiations on outstanding issues within seven days; (viii) sanctions imposed on Iran to be lifted in phases on Iranian compliance. Iran's ILNA and Tasnim confirmed a draft was moving (CONFIRMED PROCEDURAL ACTION — multi-source, but all trace back to same leak chain). **The text addresses everything Trump's May 16 letter did NOT center on (Hormuz, ceasefire) and OMITS what Trump's May 16 letter centered on (full dismantlement + zero enrichment)** — meaning it functions as a CEASEFIRE-AND-NORMALIZATION agreement, NOT a nuclear agreement. **The 'pakistan / framework draft within 14 days by 2026-05-30' trigger advances to PARTIALLY_ACTIVE_LEAKED_TEXT** — Al Arabiya leak is the partial deliverable. **The asymmetric path the leak text creates**: ceasefire/sanctions/Hormuz deliverable in days, nuclear deliverable foreclosed by Mojtaba directive (per the nuclear-program node), Israel-frustration framing escalates. **No public US or Iran formal acceptance by May 22 window close** — Trump pivoted publicly to 'in no hurry' framing, converting the original 2-3 day window into open-ended timing. **Rubio May 21 publicly stated (CBS) any deal would be 'unfeasible' if Iran continues pursuing Strait of Hormuz tolling system** — the leaked text's freedom-of-navigation provision is US-acceptable, but the parallel Iran-Oman 'permanent toll mechanism' discussions (per Wikipedia 2026 Hormuz crisis) create an implementation-test for the Hormuz framework. **The structural picture**: Pakistan retains official mediator role; Saudi (Al Arabiya leak channel — MbS positioning for deal credit) and Qatar (Al Jazeera 'Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire' frame — protecting deal architecture and Qatari role) are now coordinated editorial channels around the Pakistan-deliverable. The choice of Al Arabiya (Saudi) rather than Al Jazeera (Qatar) for the leak is itself intelligence — MbS positioning for primary deal-credit recipient even though Pakistan is formal mediator. PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **A2 NAQVI-PEZESHKIAN 90-MIN PRIVATE MEETING SUNDAY MAY 17 — PAKISTAN-NAQVI MEDIATION DAY 2 OF 14 FRAMEWORK-DELIVERABLE TRIGGER WINDOW; ACTION UPGRADE FROM RHETORIC.** Pakistan Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi held 90-minute private meeting with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian Sunday at Presidential Administration complex in Tehran (total visit ~3 hours; meeting photographed and documented). Readout per Pakistan Today + Daily Pakistan + Arab News + Express Tribune: Naqvi 'praising Pakistan's role in stabilising a fragile ceasefire and discussing prospects for restarting US-Iran peace negotiations.' Pezeshkian: 'an ideal opportunity to deepen ties' + offered to expand trade, scientific cooperation, academic exchanges. **Naqvi framing — 'recent developments had clarified regional alignments and provided a vital baseline for future strategic decisions' — is the most operational language any mediator has used in this cycle**, signalling the channel is positioned to produce a framework deliverable, not a ceremonial photo-op. IRNA covered through Iranian semi-official framing; Iranian state outlets did not deny mediation but framed as Pakistani initiative (consistent with Iran's preference for face-saving). Israel Hayom counterframe: 'Tehran airs weapons training on TV as Pakistan rushes to mediate' = kinetic-counterframe. RFE/RL Western corroboration. **No White House public response to the meeting through Monday morning IST**. **The simultaneity is the signal**: Naqvi mediation lands on SAME WEEKEND as Trump-Netanyahu coordination + Saudi drone interception + UAE Barakah strike + Trump 'clock is ticking' = diplomatic and kinetic tracks running in clear tension, not sequence. **PRIOR (May 17): PAKISTANI INTERIOR MINISTER MOHSIN NAQVI ARRIVED TEHRAN SATURDAY MAY 16 ON 2-DAY OFFICIAL VISIT EXPLICITLY DESCRIBED IN PAKISTANI OFFICIAL COMMUNICATIONS AS 'TO FACILITATE' US-IRAN PEACE TALKS** (CONFIRMED ACTION — Daily Pakistan + Pakistan Today (two pieces) + BSS News primary). Naqvi was received in Tehran by Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni; the two reaffirmed 'shared commitment to strengthening bilateral engagement and enhancing cooperation in addressing regional security challenges.' This is **Naqvi's second consecutive visit to Iran in recent months**, following a prior trip with Pakistan Army Chief Gen. Asim Munir — a back-channel architecture that has been mediating since Feb 28 (Iran delivered its 14-point response via Pakistan in early May; Trump rejected). **The simultaneity is the signal**: Naqvi's mediation visit lands on the SAME WEEKEND as Trump's 'Calm Before the Storm' Truth Social post and NYT's renewed-strike preparation report. Pakistan is operationally inserting itself as the active mediator while the US is publicly hardening — consistent with Pakistan's incentive structure (avoid regional war that would devastate its energy economy and Gulf remittance corridor) and consistent with Iran's preference for an Asian mediation track over the failed European one. **Pakistan-Naqvi channel is functionally the SIXTH PILLAR of Iran's diplomatic architecture** (alongside Araghchi defer-enrichment, Shamkhani conditional-acceptance, Pezeshkian never-bow, Akraminia surprising-options, IRGC drill closed). The choreography refinement is structurally important because the more institutional pillars are running in parallel, the harder it is for the US to extract a single binding Iranian commitment within Trump's 2-month compliance window. **The diplomatic and kinetic tracks are now running in clear tension, not sequence — Trump can simultaneously prepare strikes and hold an open mediation channel.** RFE/RL separately reported Iran delivered a new negotiation plan via Pakistan in early May (already in yesterday's frame); Naqvi's in-person follow-up is operational, not just rhetorical. No published US administration response yet; US side will surface in coming 48 hours. **PRIOR (May 13):** Day 73-74 (Mon-Tue). **A1 IRAN DELIVERED FORMAL WRITTEN MULTI-PAGE RESPONSE TO US VIA PAKISTAN OVER MAY 10-11.** Iran's response went via PAKISTAN — NOT via Qatar-IRGC channel — per public reporting (intel A1). 'Iran responds via Qatar-IRGC channel rather than Pakistan-FM channel within 7 days' trigger added May 10 is at Day 2 of 7, NOT triggered. Pakistan retains operational mediation primacy on this specific formal-response transmission. **A12 SAUDI FM PRINCE FAISAL BIN FARHAN CALLED PAKISTANI FM ISHAQ DAR MAY 11 TO DISCUSS 'PAKISTAN'S MEDIATION EFFORTS'** (also separately called Iranian FM Araghchi same day). Saudi positioning as diplomatic underwriter. Pakistan source: 'no immediate danger' of war resumption; back-channels active despite public harshness. **TRUMP PUBLICLY REJECTED IRAN'S RESPONSE AS 'TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE' / 'GARBAGE' / CEASEFIRE 'ON MASSIVE LIFE SUPPORT' — Iran's response transmitted via Pakistan, Pakistan's diplomatic capital not yet damaged but the framework Pakistan ferried is now in rejection state.** Trump China visit May 14-15 confirmed = pause window during 1-week clock close. **B8 PM MODI 5-NATION TOUR MAY 15-20** does NOT include Pakistan or Iran but DOES include UAE (only third combatant per WSJ Lavan revelation B6) — Indian strategic-autonomy posture relative to Pakistan-mediation track continues. Pakistan's public-facing role unchanged: Pakistani FO spokesperson continues briefing optimistic framing. Multi-channel architecture (Pakistan official + Qatar substantive + Saudi underwriter + Russia uranium-custodian standing offer) is now publicly visible and stable. — PAKISTAN RETAINS OFFICIAL TITLE BUT QATAR HAS EMERGED AS THE SUBSTANTIVE IRAN-SIDE OPERATIONAL CHANNEL VIA IRGC SENIOR GENERALS.** Per Axios's Saturday May 9 scoop (Barak Ravid byline): 'While Pakistan has been the official mediator since the beginning of the war, the Qataris have been working behind the scenes... White House views them as especially effective in negotiations with Iran. Qataris functioning as one of at least three back channels' and 'using contacts with senior IRGC generals involved in Iran's decision-making' (CONFIRMED — Axios primary, Times of Israel, Newsmax, Israel National News, TASS, Tribune India). The structural picture today separates POLITICAL MEDIATION (Pakistan, the public-facing channel that Iran continues to cite via Baghaei) from OPERATIONAL MEDIATION (Qatar, the substantive Iran-side channel via IRGC). The 9-point/14-point negotiation may be running on TWO PARALLEL TRACKS with different counterparties. **Pakistan's public-facing role unchanged in form:** Pakistani FO spokesperson Tahir Andrabi continues briefing that Pakistan 'expects an agreement sooner rather than later' (CONFIRMED — Washington Times, Arab News PK, NPR); Iran continues to reference 'Pakistani mediators' as the channel through which it will relay its formal response (Baghaei via IRNA/Tasnim). Saudi May 9 statement endorsing Pakistan's mediation (Radio Pakistan, Arab News) is consistent with the public-facing narrative but not necessarily with the substantive channel. **Iran has historically preferred Oman/Qatar back-channels over Pakistan** despite recent Pakistan-Iran ties — the Axios revelation aligns with that historical pattern. Trump May 7-8 publicly endorsed Pakistan mediation + disclosed Pakistan asked US 'not to militarily engage Iran during negotiations' — the diplomatic-constraint narrative remains operative, but it now describes the POLITICAL-mediation envelope, not the SUBSTANTIVE-negotiation channel. PRIOR FRAMING (preserved): Pakistan's mediation produced (a) May 5 pause framework, (b) 14-point MOU drafting, (c) explicit 'both sides lift Hormuz blockades' provision. If Iran formally signs by May 14 or May 21, Pakistan emerges as structural winner of the public-facing architecture; the Axios-disclosed Qatar-IRGC channel emerges as structural winner of the substantive negotiation.

active_triggers.condition: Pakistan hosts direct US-Iran face-to-face talks
Pakistan-brokered Iranian unified proposal delivered to US within 21 days (Munir-Sharif channel)

active_triggers.resolution_note: Al Arabiya late May 21 leaked what it called the Pakistan-mediated 'final draft' — ceasefire + Hormuz freedom-of-navigation + phased sanctions lift + 7-day talks window. Trigger advances to PARTIALLY_ACTIVE_LEAKED_TEXT: a draft IS now in leak architecture (REPORTED via Al Arabiya primary), but NO public US/Iran formal acceptance by window close. Final resolution awaits whether the draft converts to formal announcement or collapses back into kinetic-binary.

recent_signals.headline: **NO NEW DEVELOPMENT MAY 28 — ABRAHAM ACCORDS STATE OF PLAY UNCHANGED.** Pakistan has issued no new public response since DefMin Asif's May 26 on-the-record rejection of the Accords ('clashes with our fundamental ideologies'). The rejection stands; the Munir COAS mediation track and the Qatar dual-channel continue underneath. Carry-forward only — no fabricated event.
sources: Intel dossier 2026-05-28 A8 (Abraham Accords — no new Pakistan response; May 26 rejection unchanged)
recent_signals.verification: No new Pakistan public response since May 26 rejection = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (carry-forward); state of play unchanged.

recent_signals.headline: **PAKISTAN'S DEFENCE MINISTER KHAWAJA MUHAMMAD ASIF PUBLICLY REJECTED JOINING THE ABRAHAM ACCORDS — 'CLASHES WITH OUR FUNDAMENTAL IDEOLOGIES.' FIRST CONFIRMED ACCEPT/REJECT OF TRUMP'S MANDATORY-ACCORDS CONDITION; THE TRIGGER FIRES ON THE REJECTION LIMB. TRUMP HIT BACK ('SHOULD NOT BE PART OF THIS DEAL' / 'SHOWS BAD INTENTION'); LEADERS REPORTEDLY 'STUNNED'; THE NAMED-NATION LIST WIDENED TO EIGHT.** (1) **Pakistan's flat public rejection [CONFIRMED diplomatic action]**: DefMin Asif told Samaa TV that joining the Accords 'clashes with our fundamental ideologies' — a flat public 'no' on the record, consistent with Pakistan's long-standing two-state position and its declining the Accords under Imran Khan. **This is a CONFIRMED diplomatic action, not mere silence/slow-walk — Islamabad has said no.** (2) **Trump's response [CONFIRMED RHETORIC; REPORTED reaction]**: in May 26 Truth Social posts Trump said the signatories 'should not be part of this Deal,' that refusal 'shows bad intention,' and told Saudi Arabia and Qatar to 'go first.' Per a US official cited by Axios the leaders on the weekend call — 'especially those of Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Pakistan' — 'appeared stunned' (single Axios channel). Qatar has not publicly responded; Saudi silent; UAE/MbZ the lone supporter (already a signatory). (3) **List widened to eight [CONFIRMED]**: May 26 reporting widens the named-nation list from six (Saudi, Qatar, Pakistan, Egypt, Jordan, Turkey) to EIGHT, adding UAE and Bahrain (both already signatories) — the demand is now 'all of these countries simultaneously.' (4) **Strategic read**: the rejection raises the activation energy for any signature and hands reluctant capitals a fresh reason to slow-walk the Iran deal. The mandatory-Accords friction coexists with Pakistan's substantive deliverable — the Munir COAS Tehran mediation track and the Qatar dual-channel (Qatar hosted the Ghalibaf/Araghchi/Hemmati delegation in Doha, now concluded). The Pakistan-framework-draft trigger holds at partially_active_leaked_text (by 2026-05-30).
sources: Samaa TV via reporting (DefMin Khawaja Asif Accords rejection — Pakistani broadcaster primary)
Trump Truth Social May 26 ('should not be part of this Deal' / 'shows bad intention' / Saudi+Qatar 'go first')
Axios (leaders 'appeared stunned' — single Axios channel on the reaction)
Time May 26 ('What to Know About the Abraham Accords as Trump Seeks Iran Deal' — eight-nation widening)
Jerusalem Post / KSAT-AP May 25-26 (Accords linkage + additional countries)
The Week / ThePrint (Pakistan two-state / declined-Accords-under-Imran-Khan context)
recent_signals.verification: Pakistan DefMin Asif Accords rejection ('clashes with our fundamental ideologies') = CONFIRMED diplomatic action (Samaa TV primary, multi-outlet); Trump 'should not be part of this Deal' / 'shows bad intention' / Saudi+Qatar 'go first' = CONFIRMED RHETORIC (Trump Truth Social May 26); leaders 'appeared stunned' = REPORTED (single Axios channel); list widened six→eight (UAE + Bahrain added) = CONFIRMED (Time/JPost/KSAT-AP); coexistence with Munir mediation track + Qatar dual-channel = CONFIRMED carry-forward; Pakistan-framework-draft trigger holds at partially_active_leaked_text.

recent_signals.headline: **TRUMP NAMED PAKISTAN AMONG SIX MUSLIM NATIONS 'MANDATORILY' REQUIRED TO SIGN THE ABRAHAM ACCORDS AS PART OF THE IRAN DEAL — A 'DIFFICULT POSITION' FOR ISLAMABAD; RESPONSE = SILENCE / SLOW-WALK. NO ACCEPTANCE.** (1) **Mandatory-Accords demand [RHETORIC — CONFIRMED Trump posts + multi-outlet]**: in Monday Truth Social posts Trump declared it 'should be mandatory' that six named Muslim nations — including Pakistan — 'simultaneously' sign onto the Abraham Accords, telling Saudi and Qatar to 'go first' and the rest to 'follow suit.' (2) **Pakistan reaction = silence / slow-walk [REPORTED — Axios single-sourcing on the reaction]**: per Axios's Barak Ravid the leaders 'especially those of Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Pakistan, who don't have formal diplomatic relations with Israel, were surprised… there was silence on the line.' Pakistan historically backs a two-state solution and declined the Accords under Imran Khan (The Week, ThePrint) — Islamabad is placed in a structurally difficult position relative to its mediator role. **Encode as watching/slow-walk; NO Pakistani acceptance.** (3) **Coexists with the substantive Pakistan deliverable**: the Munir COAS Tehran mediation track (concluded May 23) and dual-channel architecture with Qatar (whose delegation is now in Doha hosting Iran's Ghalibaf/Araghchi/Hemmati on Hormuz + HEU + frozen assets). The mandatory-Accords condition vastly increases the deal's degrees of freedom and hands reluctant capitals — Pakistan included — a reason to slow-walk. (4) **Pakistan-framework-draft trigger holds at partially_active_leaked_text (Day 9 of 14, by 2026-05-30)** — Trump's Monday 'no rush' framing operationally extends the timeline past the window.
sources: Axios (Barak Ravid) May 24 (mandatory-Accords demand + 'silence on the line' incl. Pakistan)
NPR / Washington Post / CNBC May 25 (Accords linkage)
Times of Israel May 25 ('go first' framing)
The Week / ThePrint (Pakistan two-state / declined-Accords-under-Imran-Khan context)
recent_signals.verification: Trump mandatory-Accords demand naming Pakistan = CONFIRMED RHETORIC (Trump posts + multi-outlet); 'silence on the line' reaction incl. Pakistan = REPORTED (Axios single-sourcing); Pakistan two-state / declined-Accords-under-Imran-Khan historical position = CONFIRMED (The Week/ThePrint) — placed in 'difficult position,' NO acceptance, slow-walk; coexistence with Munir mediation track + Qatar dual-channel = CONFIRMED carry-forward.

recent_signals.headline: **PAKISTAN'S COAS FIELD MARSHAL ASIM MUNIR VISITED TEHRAN MAY 22-23 ON ~24-HOUR MEDIATION TRIP — MEDIATION TRACK STRUCTURALLY ESCALATED FROM INTERIOR MINISTER NAQVI TO MILITARY-CHIEF LEVEL. ISPR: 'ENCOURAGING PROGRESS TOWARDS A FINAL UNDERSTANDING.' IRAN COUNTER-SIGNAL: GHALIBAF 'US NOT HONEST PARTNER' + IRAN UN MISSION 'EXCESSIVE DEMANDS' + TASNIM 'IRAN PREPARING FOR RESUMING FIGHTING.'** (1) Munir met Iranian President Pezeshkian + Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf + FM Araghchi + Interior Minister Momeni in Tehran (CONFIRMED ACTION — Al Jazeera May 22-23 primary, ARY News, Pakistan Observer/ISPR primary, Geo TV, Daily Pakistan, Times of Israel). (2) ISPR readout described discussions as 'short but highly productive' with 'encouraging progress towards a final understanding' on ending the war (REPORTED — ISPR primary). (3) **Structural escalation**: through May 22 Pakistan's interface was Interior Minister Naqvi (civilian-government channel); Munir's COAS-level visit promotes the channel to Pakistan's most powerful single actor and the military-to-military axis Tehran institutionally trusts more than the civilian government. Vali Nasr's framing — Pakistan's role is 'unprecedented' and the Islamabad Talks were 'the most substantial direct engagement' — now operationally reinforced. (4) **Dual-channel architecture operationally explicit**: Al Jazeera May 23 quoted an Iranian official describing Qatar's role as 'vital, important, and fundamental to supporting Pakistan's mediation' — Pakistan formal/military + Qatar substantive/IRGC backchannel is the now-explicit architecture. (5) **Saudi FM Faisal bin Farhan publicly praised Trump diplomacy + Pakistan mediation** + urged Iran to 'seize the opportunity to avoid escalation' (Al Arabiya May 22-23 primary) — Gulf editorial coordination intact, Saudi positioning for primary deal-credit. (6) **Iranian institutional counter-signal at maximum amplitude while Munir is in Tehran**: FM Baghaei 'deep and significant disagreements remain' (Al Jazeera primary, IRNA); Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf publicly said US 'not an honest negotiating partner'; Iran UN mission accused Washington of 'excessive demands'; Tasnim (military source primary) reported 'Iran is preparing for resuming fighting with the United States and Israel.' (7) Iran FM spokesman Baghaei publicly framed deal timeline as '30-60 days' for finalizing MOU details (Al Jazeera May 23 primary, IRNA) — first publicly-articulated Iranian-side timeline of the cycle, structurally LONGER than Trump's 'every day better' framing or Al Arabiya leak's 'within 7 days' architecture. (8) **Pakistan-framework-draft trigger (Day 4 of 14, by 2026-05-30) holds at partially_active_leaked_text** — no formal US/Iran acceptance through May 23 close.
sources: Al Jazeera May 23 'Munir, Rubio say progress made in US-Iran talks, Tehran signals caution' — primary
Al Jazeera May 22 'Pakistan's army chief in Iran as US's Rubio says slight progress in talks' — primary

Times of Israel May 23 liveblog — primary
Haaretz May 23 liveblog (Tasnim military source primary)

recent_signals.verification: Munir COAS Tehran visit May 22-23 + multi-meeting roster = CONFIRMED ACTION (multi-source primary); ISPR 'encouraging progress' framing = REPORTED (ISPR primary, not independently corroborated by Iranian side); Ghalibaf + UN mission + Baghaei + Tasnim Iranian counter-signal fanout = CONFIRMED RHETORIC; Saudi FM endorsement = CONFIRMED RHETORIC (Al Arabiya primary, Saudi state-aligned); Baghaei 30-60 days timeline = CONFIRMED RHETORIC; no formal US/Iran acceptance through May 23 = CONFIRMED ABSENCE.

recent_signals.headline: **AL ARABIYA LATE MAY 21 LEAKED THE PAKISTAN-MEDIATED 'FINAL DRAFT' TEXT — CEASEFIRE + HORMUZ FREEDOM-OF-NAVIGATION + PHASED SANCTIONS LIFT + TALKS WITHIN 7 DAYS, NO URANIUM DISMANTLEMENT. NAQVI-ARAGHCHI MULTIPLE TEHRAN MEETINGS MAY 20-22. PAKISTAN MEDIATION HAS PRODUCED ITS FIRST OPERATIONAL DELIVERABLE — A LEAK-ARCHITECTURE DRAFT TEXT.** (1) **Al Arabiya English May 22 (primary leak — Saudi state-influenced)**: Pakistan-mediated 'final draft,' 'could be announced within hours,' core elements (i) immediate, comprehensive, unconditional ceasefire on land/sea/air; (ii) mutual commitment not to target military/civilian/economic infrastructure; (iii) end to military operations + halt to 'media war'; (iv) commitments to respect sovereignty, territorial integrity, non-interference; (v) joint monitoring mechanism for ceasefire implementation; (vi) Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz freedom-of-navigation guarantees; (vii) talks on outstanding issues within 7 days; (viii) sanctions imposed on Iran lifted in phases on Iranian compliance. (2) **Naqvi-Araghchi meetings May 20-22 (CONFIRMED — Washington Times May 22 + IRNA primary)** in Tehran across multiple occasions, more operational than the original May 16-17 visit. (3) **Iran's ILNA and Tasnim confirmed a draft was moving** (CONFIRMED PROCEDURAL ACTION — but all wire chains trace to same Al Arabiya leak chain; counts as ONE source per protocol). (4) **The text addresses everything Trump's May 16 letter did NOT center on (Hormuz, ceasefire) and OMITS what Trump's May 16 letter centered on (full dismantlement + zero enrichment)** — it functions as a CEASEFIRE-AND-NORMALIZATION agreement, NOT a nuclear agreement. (5) **Pakistan-framework-draft trigger** (added May 19, watching by 2026-05-30) advances to PARTIALLY_ACTIVE_LEAKED_TEXT — the Al Arabiya leak is the partial deliverable. (6) **No public US or Iran formal acceptance by May 22 window close** — Trump pivoted publicly to 'in no hurry' framing. **Three-Gulf-leader editorial coordination operationally active**: Al Arabiya (Saudi — deal-text leak channel; MbS positioning for deal credit) + Al Jazeera (Qatar — 'Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire' framing protects Qatari role + channels deal-spoiler narrative onto Israel) + The National (UAE — restraint, preserves Barakah-attack mediation space). **The choice of Al Arabiya (Saudi) rather than Al Jazeera (Qatar) for the leak is itself intelligence**: MbS positioning Saudi as primary deal-credit recipient even though Pakistan is formal mediator and Qatar is operational backchannel. **The mediation thesis is at maximum operational expression**: Pakistan's diplomatic equity peak of the cycle. **Asymmetric path the leak text creates**: ceasefire deliverable in days, nuclear deliverable foreclosed by Mojtaba directive (per nuclear-program node). **Risk to Pakistan's standing**: if no formal public announcement materializes within 7 days, Al Arabiya leak becomes 'mediation effort that didn't land' — Pakistan absorbs reputation cost.
sources: Al Arabiya English May 22 (Pakistan-mediated final draft text primary leak — Saudi state-influenced)
Washington Times May 22 (Naqvi-Araghchi Tehran meeting confirmation)
IRNA (Iranian semi-official primary on Naqvi-Araghchi meetings)
ILNA / Tasnim (Iran confirmation draft moving — wire chain traces to Al Arabiya)
Pravda USA aggregator citing Al Arabiya / ILNA / Tasnim
recent_signals.verification: Pakistan-mediated draft text leak = REPORTED (Al Arabiya primary; multi-outlet wire chain traces back to same leak — counts as ONE source per protocol); Naqvi-Araghchi multiple Tehran meetings May 20-22 = CONFIRMED ACTION (Washington Times + IRNA); ceasefire-without-uranium architecture = INFERRED structural reading based on text content; no public US/Iran formal acceptance by May 22 close = CONFIRMED ABSENCE.

recent_signals.headline: **PAKISTAN MEDIATION DAY 3 OF 14 — TRUMP'S POSTPONEMENT POST EXPLICITLY CREDITS THE GULF LEADERS BUT IMPLICITLY VALIDATES PAKISTAN CHANNEL ('serious negotiations are now taking place'). PM SHEHBAZ SHARIF CALLED IT 'A SHINING MOMENT IN HISTORY.'** Trump's postponement post late May 18 US time credited Qatar Emir Tamim, Saudi MbS, and UAE MbZ by name; Pakistan was not publicly named but the mediation channel — operationally activated by Naqvi's two-day Tehran visit May 16-17 (90-min Pezeshkian meeting + Momeni/Ghalibaf meetings) — is the substantive procedural channel through which Iran's counter-proposal is moving. **Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif publicly praised the mediation as 'a shining moment in history' Monday May 18** — Islamabad positioning to take public credit for the postponement and signaling that Pakistan sees the mediation as a strategic equity play. Sputnik Global (TASS-adjacent) reported the Naqvi-Pezeshkian talks 'covered mediation, border security, and new trade corridors' — Pakistan extracting bilateral value from the mediator role, NOT just functioning as neutral conduit. **Al Jazeera analytical piece 'Pakistan's mediation faces limits as Iran-US tensions deepen' (May 18)**: Pakistan's mediation is structurally constrained — Qatar's mediation muscle is stronger institutionally (Doha-process precedent, US base presence, Iran communications), but Pakistan has Iran-side trust and Sunni-Islamic legitimacy. **The Pakistan channel is procedural-real, framework-draft-pending** — no published framework draft has surfaced through Tuesday morning IST. Trump's 'very close to making a deal' framing in the postponement post and Iran's far-apart Baghaei-articulated counter-proposal (asset unfreezing + sanctions lift + blockade end + war reparations + Lebanon cessation) suggest the channel is moving paper but the substantive gap remains significant. **The postponement extends rather than resolves the structural deadlock.** Sadjadpour 'two hubris' frame applied to third party: Pakistan's institutional incentive runs partly counter to a fast deliverable — every additional day of mediation extends Pakistan's diplomatic standing.
sources: Pakistan Today / Daily Pakistan (primary on Shehbaz framing)
recent_signals.verification: Naqvi-Pezeshkian operational channel = CONFIRMED ACTION (multi-source); Shehbaz 'shining moment' = CONFIRMED RHETORIC (Pakistan Today primary); Trump's implicit validation via 'serious negotiations are now taking place' = CONFIRMED (Trump postponement post); no published framework draft = CONFIRMED ABSENCE; Iran's far-apart counter-proposal demands = CONFIRMED (Tasnim Baghaei statement).

recent_signals.headline: **NAQVI-PEZESHKIAN 90-MIN PRIVATE MEETING SUNDAY MAY 17 — PAKISTAN-NAQVI MEDIATION DAY 2 OF 14 FRAMEWORK-DELIVERABLE TRIGGER WINDOW; ACTION UPGRADE FROM RHETORIC.** Pakistan Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi held 90-min private meeting with Iranian President Pezeshkian Sunday at Presidential Administration complex in Tehran; total visit ~3 hours; meeting photographed and documented. Pakistan Today, Daily Pakistan, Arab News, Express Tribune readout: 'praising Pakistan's role in stabilising a fragile ceasefire and discussing prospects for restarting US-Iran peace negotiations.' Pezeshkian: 'an ideal opportunity to deepen ties' + offered trade/scientific/academic expansion. **Naqvi framing — 'recent developments had clarified regional alignments and provided a vital baseline for future strategic decisions' — is the most operational language any mediator has used in this cycle**; signalling the channel is positioned to produce a framework deliverable, not ceremonial photo-op. **Trigger: 'Pakistan-Naqvi mediation produces published framework draft within 14 days (by 2026-05-30)' at Day 2 of 14 with channel demonstrably operational.** IRNA covered via Iranian semi-official framing (Pakistani initiative); Iranian state outlets did not deny mediation but preserved face-saving. Israel Hayom counterframe: 'Tehran airs weapons training on TV as Pakistan rushes to mediate' — kinetic counter-perspective. RFE/RL via globalsecurity.org Western corroboration. **No White House public response through Monday morning IST.** **Simultaneity is the signal**: Naqvi mediation lands on SAME weekend as Trump-Netanyahu coordination + Saudi drone interception (B1) + UAE Barakah perimeter strike + Trump 'clock is ticking' — diplomatic + kinetic tracks running in tension, not sequence. **Markets cascade fires Monday**: Brent $111.47 +2.0% (StanChart $130-145 path activates); USD/INR ₹96.29 record (Day 6 of 5-7 trigger fires); Sensex/Nifty open -1.30%/-1.04%. The Tuesday May 19 Trump NSC meeting is the operational binary — if Trump issues strike orders Tuesday, Pakistan's diplomatic capital is publicly tested; if Trump produces a framework deliverable, the Naqvi channel emerges as structural winner.
sources: Pakistan Today May 18 'Pakistan Intensifies Iran-US Mediation as Naqvi Holds Crucial Talks With President Pezeshkian'
Daily Pakistan May 16 'Naqvi in Tehran in Pakistan's push to revive stalled US-Iran Talks'
Arab News May 17 'Pakistan interior minister meets Iranian counterpart in Tehran'
recent_signals.verification: Naqvi-Pezeshkian 90-min meeting + 3-hour visit = CONFIRMED ACTION (Pakistan Today + Daily Pakistan + Arab News + Express Tribune multi-source primary; meeting photographed and documented); IRNA coverage = CONFIRMED Iranian semi-official framing; No White House response = CONFIRMED ABSENCE through Monday morning IST; Naqvi 'baseline for future strategic decisions' framing = CONFIRMED official communication.

recent_signals.headline: **PAKISTANI INTERIOR MINISTER MOHSIN NAQVI ARRIVED TEHRAN SATURDAY MAY 16 ON 2-DAY OFFICIAL VISIT EXPLICITLY DESCRIBED IN PAKISTANI OFFICIAL COMMUNICATIONS AS 'TO FACILITATE' US-IRAN PEACE TALKS** (CONFIRMED ACTION — Daily Pakistan + Pakistan Today (two pieces) + BSS News primary). Naqvi was received in Tehran by Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni; the two reaffirmed 'shared commitment to strengthening bilateral engagement and enhancing cooperation in addressing regional security challenges.' Second consecutive Naqvi visit to Iran in recent months, following a prior trip with Pakistan Army Chief Gen. Asim Munir. **The simultaneity is the signal**: Naqvi's mediation visit lands on the SAME WEEKEND as Trump's 'Calm Before the Storm' Truth Social post and NYT's renewed-strike preparation report. Pakistan is operationally inserting itself as the active mediator while the US is publicly hardening. **Pakistan-Naqvi channel functions as the SIXTH PILLAR of Iran's diplomatic architecture** alongside Araghchi defer-enrichment, Shamkhani conditional-acceptance, Pezeshkian never-bow, Akraminia surprising-options, IRGC drill closed. The more institutional pillars run in parallel, the harder it is for the US to extract a single binding Iranian commitment within Trump's 2-month compliance window. **Pakistan-Naqvi visit IS being covered by Iranian semi-official media as a Pakistani mediation initiative** — Iran is publicly acknowledging the diplomatic channel without conceding ground on enrichment. No published US administration response over the weekend.
sources: Daily Pakistan May 16 'Naqvi in Tehran in Pakistan's push to revive stalled US-Iran Talks' — Pakistani primary
Pakistan Today May 16 (two pieces — 'Mohsin Naqvi lands in Tehran' + 'Pakistan, Iran Reaffirm Regional Stability')
BSS News (Bangladesh state wire) 'Pakistani minister arrives in Tehran to facilitate US-Iran peace talks' — third-country amplification — independent
recent_signals.verification: Pakistan-Naqvi Tehran visit = CONFIRMED ACTION (Daily Pakistan + Pakistan Today + BSS News multi-source primary; Iranian Interior Minister Momeni received Naqvi publicly); explicit 'to facilitate' US-Iran peace talks framing = CONFIRMED via Pakistani official communications; sixth-pillar framing = CONFIRMED structural observation (carryforward from intel.md A2/B3 framing).

recent_signals.headline: PAKISTAN'S PRIMACY AS SOLE MEDIATOR SUBORDINATED TO MULTI-CHANNEL STRUCTURE — PAKISTAN RETAINS OFFICIAL TITLE; QATAR EMERGES AS SUBSTANTIVE IRAN-SIDE OPERATIONAL CHANNEL VIA IRGC SENIOR GENERALS PER AXIOS PRIMARY SCOOP. Pakistani FO spokesperson Andrabi continues 'expects agreement sooner rather than later' framing (CONFIRMED multi-source). Iran continues referencing 'Pakistani mediators' through Baghaei. Saudi May 9 endorsement of Pakistan mediation via Radio Pakistan/Arab News reinforces Pakistan's public-facing primacy but not the substantive channel. The political vs operational mediation now runs on TWO PARALLEL TRACKS: Political (Pakistan + public-facing FM channel) and Operational (Qatar→IRGC senior generals — most-trusted by White House per Axios). Iran has historically preferred Oman/Qatar back-channels over Pakistan; Axios revelation aligns with that pattern. The diplomatic-constraint narrative ('Pakistan asked US not to militarily engage Iran during negotiations,' Trump May 7-8) remains operative but describes the POLITICAL-mediation envelope, not the SUBSTANTIVE-negotiation channel.
sources: Times of Israel live blog May 9-10
Radio Pakistan (Saudi endorsement of Pakistan mediation)
recent_signals.verification: Axios scoop on Qatar-IRGC channel = REPORTED (Axios primary, multi-corroborated; Iran does not confirm or deny publicly); Pakistan public-facing primacy (Andrabi/Baghaei references) = CONFIRMED multi-source; Saudi public endorsement of Pakistan mediation = CONFIRMED via Radio Pakistan / Arab News

recent_signals.headline: TRUMP PUBLICLY ENDORSED PAKISTAN MEDIATION + DISCLOSED PAKISTAN ASKED US 'NOT TO MILITARILY ENGAGE IRAN' DURING NEGOTIATIONS — FIRST PUBLIC TRUMP DESCRIPTION OF PAKISTAN AS ACTIVE DIPLOMATIC CONSTRAINT ON US MILITARY OPERATIONS. Trump May 7-8: talks 'going well,' Tehran wants peace agreement 'a lot more than I do'; praised 'Pakistani civilian and military leaders for their mediation in the conflict.' Consistent with the pattern of the May 7 destroyer-attack response being limited to port strikes (no Iranian leadership targeting, no IRGC HQ targeting, no nuclear-infrastructure targeting). FM Baghaei reiterated Friday Iran would relay response 'to Pakistani mediators when complete.' Pakistani parliamentary spokesperson Rezaei publicly called US text 'more of an American wishlist than a reality' — Pakistani-side dissent visible inside the channel, possibly Iran-aligned parliamentary faction views distinct from Sharif-Munir executive line. Saudi Al-Hadath denial endorsed 'Pakistan's efforts to broker a peace agreement' — Pakistan now has Saudi public backing on mediation track.
recent_signals.verification: Trump-Pakistan diplomatic-constraint disclosure = CONFIRMED RHETORIC/SIGNAL (Trump primary, Pakistan Today multi-source); Baghaei Pakistan-mediator framing = CONFIRMED multi-source; Rezaei dissent = CONFIRMED multi-source; Saudi public endorsement of Pakistan via Al-Hadath = CONFIRMED

recent_signals.headline: PAKISTAN MEDIATION DELIVERED 14-POINT MOU TEXT — Witkoff and Kushner negotiating directly with Iranian officials AND through Pakistani mediators. Pakistan PM Shehbaz: 'grateful to Trump for courageous leadership.' Russia uranium-custodian role potentially REACTIVATED for HEU custody under MOU (Peskov reaffirmed offer 'remains active' but US has rejected; alternatives IAEA, Kazakhstan). Architecture: Pakistan PRIMARY, supported by direct Witkoff/Kushner channel.
recent_signals.verification: MOU framework = REPORTED multi-corroborated; Pakistan mediation channel = CONFIRMED ACTION (cited in MOU framework, PM Shehbaz public statement); Russia uranium offer 'still active' = CONFIRMED Russia-side, but US has rejected
